column3: 2026 Lunar New Year Gala
column6: The webpage provides the Chinese Students and Scholars Association at the University of Pittsburgh's announcement of the 2026 Spring Festival Gala, held at Bellefield Hall on February 14, 2026. The announcement invites the Chinese community to the festival's celebration and includes a Google Map to the event location and an event poster in Chinese for advertising. The event poster includes the event name, festival celebration wishes, event time and location, and a QR code linking to the Chinese Students and Scholars Association's WeChat account homepage.
column7: Chinese Students and Scholars Association University of Pittsburgh

column3: 2026 Chinese New Year Celebration
column6: The event poster advertises that the Pittsburgh Chinese School held the 2026 Chinese New Year Celebration at Allderdic High School on February 15, 2026. It includes an activity schedule, event date, and location.

column3: Chinatown Inn Restaurant
column6: The photograph captures the interior of Chinatown Inn Restaurant.

column3: Pittsburgh's Chinatown: A Study of Chinese Diaspora Archaeology in Pittsburgh
column6: The Master's thesis examines the Chinese community in Pittsburgh's Chinatown and the transformation of Pittsburhg Chinatwon between 1900 and 1950.

column3: An Inquiry in to Chinese Alumni Attitudes Toward Their University of Pittsburgh Experience
column6: The dissertation examines the University of Pittsburgh's Chinese alumni, including undergraduate and graduate students, overall satisfaction with their university experience, and future engagement with the university.

column3: The Chinese in Pittsburgh, A Changing Minority Community in the United States
column6: The dissertation examines the Chinese community's history in Pittsburgh from two periods. The first period is from 1872, when the first Chinese immigrated to the Pittsburgh, to 1945, when World War II ended, and the second period is from 1946 to 1978.

column3: The Last Mayor of Chinatown
column6: The video tells the story of Yuen Yee, the last honorary mayor of Pittsburgh Chinatown, through images, documents, and artifacts.

column3: Remembering Pittsburhg's Chinatown
column6: The video documents that the Chinatown Inn restaurant was officially designated as a historic landmark of Pittsburgh's historic Chinatown in 2022, in recognition of the Chinese community's contribution to the history of Pittsburgh.

column3: Uncovering Pittsburgh’s long-hidden Asian American history made me feel at home — and I learned I wasn’t alone
column6: The article is narrated in the author's first-person perspective to explore the hidden history of Pittsburgh's Chinese community, from historic Chinatown to the Chinese section of Homewood Cemetery.

column3: 2026 Year of the Horse Lunar New Year Gala — Xiaobo Waist Drum Group
column6: The video records the Xiaobo Waist Drum group's performance at the 2026 Year of the Horse Lunar New Year Gala, hosted by the Pittsburgh Chinese Cultural Center at the Pittsburgh Playhouse on February 7, 2026.

column3: Chinese-American Festival Debuts in Oakland
column6: The news article reports that the first Pittsburgh Chinese Cultural Festival, aimed to promoting Chinese culture in Pittsburgh and strengthening connections within the Chinese community, as expressed by the event organizers, was held on Bigelow Boulevard.

column3: Chinese Mid-Autumn and Global Food Festival
column6: The webpage announces the Chinese Mid-Autumn Festival celebration held at the Cohon University Center in the Careneige Mellon University on October 1, 2025. The announcement notes that the event is free and open to the public and includes the event time, location, and festival activities.

column3: Lunar New Year Parade held in Squirrel Hill Brings Hope, Happiness and Togetherness
column6: The news article reports the Chinese New Year celebration parade in the streets of Squirrel Hill, Pittsburgh.

column3: 2026 Year of the Horse Lunar New Year Gala — Erhu Solo: Horse Racing, Guanzi and Erhu Ensemble: A Love Before Time
column6: The video records an Erhu solo performance of the piece Horse Racing, and a Guanzi and Erhu ensemble featuring the piece A Love Before Time at the 2026 Year of the Horse Lunar New Year Gala, hosted by the Pittsburgh Chinese Cultural Center at the Pittsburgh Playhouse on February 7, 2026.

column3: A History of Pittsburgh’s Chinese Community by Lydia Ott
column6: The video records the speech given by Lydia Ott, a doctoral student in occupational therapy at the University of Pittsburgh and producer of the documentary Pittsburgh’s Lost Chinatown, and her grandmother, Lydia Lee Ott, about the history of the Pittsburgh Chinese community.

column3: Student-run news channel seeks to connect Chinese international students with Pitt
column6: The news article reports that a Chinese international student at the University of Pittsburgh created a channel–Pitt-China, on the platform WeChat to connect Chinese students with their families by providing translated information, including community news and university communications during COVID-19.

column3: 2017 Chinese Culture Festival

column3: Pittsburgh Chinese School Shines at 2025 Dragon Boat Festival, Cultural Booth Draws Community Attention
column6: The news article reports that the Pittsburgh Chinese School presented a Chinese cultural booth featuring traditional Chinese crafts and ornaments at the Pittsburgh Dragon Boat Festival at North Park, Pittsburgh.

column3: Visiting opera stars highlight Pittsburgh Chinese Cultural Festival
column6: The news reports that the Pittsburgh Chinese Cultural Festival was held at Mellon Park, featuring a full day of vendor service, musical performances, and martial arts exhibitions.

column3: PCS Successfully Completes the 4th Annual Food Drive
column6: The news article reports that the Pittsburgh Chinese School completed its 4th Annual Food Drive, providing support to local communities facing rising living costs and financial hardship.
